title: Password changes bypass Django password validators
reasoning: The custom PasswordChangeForm only checks that the two new password fields match. The view then calls set_password directly, so configured Django AUTH_PASSWORD_VALIDATORS such as minimum length, common password, numeric-only, or user-attribute checks are not applied. Users can change to weak passwords that registration would normally reject.
reproduction: Submit the password-change form with the correct old password and a matching weak new password such as a very short or common password. The form validates as long as both new fields match, and the view saves it.
recommendation: Use django.contrib.auth.forms.PasswordChangeForm, or call django.contrib.auth.password_validation.validate_password(new_password, user) from the form and surface validation errors before saving.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: Users/tests.py is empty aside from the import stub, so there is no coverage for password validator enforcement.
suggestedRegressionTest: Configure a simple minimum-length password validator in the test and assert that changing to a too-short password is rejected and does not alter the user's password.
minimumFixScope: Replace or enhance the custom password-change form and add a validator-focused regression test.
